exercises.translation: This book is easy to read.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい means 'difficult to do' — it's the opposite of what's being expressed about the book.
wrong_choice_explanations.たい: たい expresses personal desire 'want to read' — it doesn't describe the book's inherent readability.
wrong_choice_explanations.ている: ている indicates ongoing action — it doesn't express the ease of reading the book.

exercises.example_sentence: この椅子は座り__そうですね。
exercises.translation: This chair looks easy to sit on.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい means 'difficult to do' — it would suggest the chair is hard to sit on, not comfortable.
wrong_choice_explanations.たい: たい expresses wanting to sit — it doesn't describe the chair's ease of use.
wrong_choice_explanations.ます: ます is a polite verb ending — it doesn't form a proper adjective describing the chair's quality.

exercises.translation: His stories are easy to listen to.

wrong_choice_explanations.にく: にく would make it 'difficult to listen to' — the opposite of what's being expressed.
wrong_choice_explanations.たく: たく is part of the tai-form expressing desire — it doesn't describe the inherent quality of his stories.
wrong_choice_explanations.てい: てい is part of the progressive form — it doesn't express the ease of listening.

exercises.example_sentence: この料理は作り__から、よく作ります。
exercises.translation: This dish is easy to make, so I make it often.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい means 'difficult to make' — if it were difficult, the person wouldn't make it often.
wrong_choice_explanations.たい: たい means 'want to make' — this doesn't explain why they make it frequently.
wrong_choice_explanations.ました: ました is past tense — it doesn't describe the inherent ease of making the dish.

exercises.example_sentence: 新しいアプリは使い__くて、便利です。
exercises.translation: The new app is easy to use and convenient.

wrong_choice_explanations.にく: にく would make it 'difficult to use' — this contradicts the positive assessment.
wrong_choice_explanations.たく: たく relates to wanting to use — it doesn't describe the app's inherent usability.
wrong_choice_explanations.てい: てい is part of the progressive form — it doesn't express ease of use.

exercises.example_sentence: この靴は歩き__そうですが、高いですね。
exercises.translation: These shoes look easy to walk in, but they're expensive.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい would mean 'difficult to walk in' — this doesn't fit the positive context before 'but expensive'.
wrong_choice_explanations.たい: たい expresses wanting to walk — it doesn't describe the shoes' walking comfort.
wrong_choice_explanations.ている: ている indicates ongoing action — it doesn't express the perceived ease of walking.

exercises.example_sentence: 漢字が覚え__ように、毎日練習しています。
exercises.translation: So that kanji will be easy to remember, I practice every day.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい means 'difficult to remember' — this contradicts the purpose of daily practice.
wrong_choice_explanations.たい: たい expresses wanting to remember — this doesn't fit the ように construction properly.
wrong_choice_explanations.ません: ません is negative polite form — it doesn't work grammatically in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: この文は理解し__いと思います。
exercises.translation: I think this sentence is easy to understand.

wrong_choice_explanations.にく: にく would make it 'difficult to understand' — this doesn't match the positive assessment.
wrong_choice_explanations.たく: たく relates to wanting to understand — it doesn't describe the sentence's inherent clarity.
wrong_choice_explanations.てい: てい is part of progressive form — it doesn't express ease of understanding.

exercises.example_sentence: 彼女の字はとても読み__です。
exercises.translation: Her handwriting is very easy to read.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくい: にくい means 'difficult to read' — this would be criticism, not praise of her handwriting.
wrong_choice_explanations.たいです: たいです expresses wanting to read — it doesn't describe the handwriting's legibility.
wrong_choice_explanations.ています: ています indicates ongoing action — it doesn't express the ease of reading her writing.

exercises.example_sentence: この問題は解き__から、宿題が早く終わりました。
exercises.translation: This problem was easy to solve, so I finished my homework quickly.

wrong_choice_explanations.にくかった: にくかった means 'was difficult to solve' — if it were difficult, the homework wouldn't finish quickly.
wrong_choice_explanations.たかった: たかった means 'wanted to solve' — this doesn't explain why the homework finished quickly.
wrong_choice_explanations.ていた: ていた is past progressive — it doesn't express the ease of solving that led to quick completion.
